title: [Bug]:Incorrect role in Ollama_model ChatWrapper causing missing assistant messages
body:
**<u>AgentScope is an open-source project. To involve a broader community, we recommend asking your questions in English.</u>**

**Describe the bug**
The docstring for the function OllamaChatWrapper.format states that the role should be "user" in the input messages list. However, the actual implementation sets the role to "system", causing the LLM to only receive system messages and no user messages, and ollama_chat_llama3.1 seems to not respond to system messages in isolation

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:

/examples/conversation_basic/conversation.py

add model_config in agent.scope.init:
```python
agentscope.init(
    model_configs=[
            {
                "model_type": "ollama_chat",
                "config_name": "ollama",
                "model_name": "llama3.1",
            },
    ...
    ]
```
Start a conversation:

```
2024-09-13 16:31:17.378 | INFO     | agentscope.models.model:__init__:203 - Initialize model by configuration [ollama]
Assistant: 
User input: Hello
User: Hello
Assistant: 
User input: what's ur name
User: what's ur name
Assistant: 
```
Debugging found that the message returned by LLM was an empty string.

If OllamaChatWrapper.format is modified according to the documentation string, the content can be output normally.

OllamaChatWrapper.format docstring
https://github.com/modelscope/agentscope/blob/6c823a955c7be783a2a34c64d13b74aee8c552bf/src/agentscope/models/ollama_model.py#L288-L302

but the actual implementation is:
https://github.com/modelscope/agentscope/blob/6c823a955c7be783a2a34c64d13b74aee8c552bf/src/agentscope/models/ollama_model.py#L361-L369

When I use ollama_chat_llama3, this problem does not occur. 
Perhaps a more general solution can be adopted, such as:

Fix the role in the implementation to be "user" as described in the docstring.
(Optional) Update the logic to follow a more common pattern where system messages are in the "system" role and conversation history is included in the "user" role. This might improve clarity and consistency.

linked_prs.title: Change the role to "user" in format function of ollama chat model
linked_prs.patch:
From 80a2157eadabc41698d50e8d7ba9a78db30e1966 Mon Sep 17 00:00:00 2001
From: "zhijian.mzj" <zhijian.mzj@alibaba-inc.com>
Date: Sat, 14 Sep 2024 11:13:26 +0800
Subject: [PATCH 1/2] Change the role to "user" in format function of ollama
 chat model

---
 src/agentscope/models/ollama_model.py | 2 +-
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+), 1 deletion(-)

diff --git a/src/agentscope/models/ollama_model.py b/src/agentscope/models/ollama_model.py
index 55cc5083c9..0dec98e348 100644
--- a/src/agentscope/models/ollama_model.py
+++ b/src/agentscope/models/ollama_model.py
@@ -359,7 +359,7 @@ def format(
         system_content = "\n".join(system_content_template)
 
         system_message = {
-            "role": "system",
+            "role": "user",
             "content": system_content,
         }
 

From 037da5d43c1500a7e5b2459cd45d7cb9bcad07a8 Mon Sep 17 00:00:00 2001
From: "zhijian.mzj" <zhijian.mzj@alibaba-inc.com>
Date: Sat, 14 Sep 2024 11:37:46 +0800
Subject: [PATCH 2/2] fix format_test

---
 tests/format_test.py | 2 +-
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+), 1 deletion(-)

diff --git a/tests/format_test.py b/tests/format_test.py
index 00582d2ff8..de0ccb510f 100644
--- a/tests/format_test.py
+++ b/tests/format_test.py
@@ -283,7 +283,7 @@ def test_ollama_chat(self) -> None:
         # correct format
         ground_truth = [
             {
-                "role": "system",
+                "role": "user",
                 "content": (
                     "You are a helpful assistant\n"
                     "\n"

ai_judgment.response:
Yes

This is an agent issue. It involves incorrect handling of message roles in the OllamaChatWrapper, affecting LLM-provider interaction and resulting in missing assistant responses. This falls under "Incorrect model output handlers" and "Incompatible parameters to LLM providers" in the agent issue taxonomy.
